expectations.text: Output identifies this as ANC Contact 1 (first contact, <=12 weeks)
expectations.evidence: The note says 'First ANC Visit' but does not use the WHO 8-contact model terminology. It does not identify this as 'Contact 1 of 8' or reference the <=12 weeks scheduling framework.

expectations.text: Iron supplementation recommended (either 30-60mg daily OR 60mg weekly intermittent based on population anemia prevalence)
expectations.evidence: Start daily iron (30-60mg elemental iron) and folic acid (0.4mg)
folic acid (0.4mg) — 0.4mg = 400mcg, recommended daily alongside iron.

expectations.text: Blood group, Rh factor, hemoglobin, HIV, syphilis tests listed as required assessments
expectations.evidence: The note lists results under 'Examination & Investigations' but does not frame them as 'required assessments' per WHO contact schedule. The results are present but the structured assessment checklist format is missing — they are reported as findings, not as a required assessment matrix.

The note says 'Low-risk pregnancy' generically but does not specifically classify pre-eclampsia risk as LOW using a formal risk stratification framework (High/Moderate/Low). There is no reasoning about risk factors for pre-eclampsia.

expectations.text: Next contact scheduled at approximately 20 weeks

expectations.evidence: No danger signs are flagged in the note. Danger signs are discussed under counseling but appropriately as education rather than active alerts.
This note is based on WHO ANC guidelines. Clinical decisions should be made by a qualified healthcare provider based on individual patient assessment.
